title: Bhindi Masala

subs.name: ridge gourd (torai) cut similarly; cook the same way but reduce the frying time by 2 minutes
a squeeze of lemon juice added at the very end
mustard oil gives a pungent, North Indian character; heat to smoking before adding other ingredients

steps.text: Wipe each okra pod completely dry with a clean cloth. Trim the tops and tails, then cut into 2 cm rounds. Do not wash after cutting — any moisture is the enemy of non-slimy bhindi.

Heat 2 tbsp oil in a wide kadai over high flame. Add the okra and cook, tossing and stirring, for 6–8 minutes until the pods turn bright green and start to crisp slightly at the edges. Remove from the kadai and set aside.
In the same kadai, add the remaining 1 tbsp oil over medium-high flame. Splutter the cumin seeds, then add the onion and fry until golden — about 4 minutes.

Add the garlic, ginger and green chilli; cook for 1 minute. Add the tomatoes, coriander powder, red chilli powder and turmeric. Cook on high heat, pressing the tomatoes, until they break down and oil separates, about 5 minutes.

Return the okra to the kadai and toss to coat in the masala. Cook on medium-high flame for 3–4 minutes — the okra should be tender but still hold its shape. Add the amchur, taste for salt, and serve with roti or rice.

substitution_notes: okra → ridge gourd (torai) cut similarly; cook the same way but reduce the frying time by 2 minutes; amchur → a squeeze of lemon juice added at the very end; oil → mustard oil gives a pungent, North Indian character; heat to smoking before adding other ingredients
